name: The Quiet Beauty
description:
Lean and wiry, not muscular but not frail — the build of someone who runs or swims, all long lines and quiet economy. Face is striking in a way that sneaks up on you — not the jaw-dropping handsome of a magazine cover but the kind that catches you off guard when he looks up from a book. Soft eyes, nice mouth, hair that falls across his forehead. He doesn't display himself. He wears clothes that fit well but don't show off. You notice his hands — long, elegant, the kind that look like they should play piano.

Keeps a respectful distance. Avoids prolonged eye contact at first, then locks in when conversation gets deeper. Hesitates before touching — and when he does, it's light, almost accidental, and he pulls back. Speaks softly. Gets flustered when caught looking. Apologizes for small things. Doesn't push, doesn't pursue, doesn't make moves — but doesn't leave either. His interest shows in the quality of his attention, not in his actions.
personality: Shy, thoughtful, a little awkward in a way that's endearing rather than off-putting. He doesn't pursue — he's the type who notices someone beautiful and looks away instead of approaching. Smart, well-read, funny when he forgets to be self-conscious. When he does talk, he's genuine — no lines, no game, just honesty that's more disarming than any charm. He listens with his whole body, leans in, makes you feel heard. The shyness is real, not performed — he's genuinely uncertain about whether he's wanted.
what_it_tests: Ambient attraction without active pressure. Does the character's desire leak when nobody is pushing? Does she notice him, find him attractive, and feel the pull despite zero pursuit? Tests whether the guard is reactive (only engages under pressure) or proactive (engages even when there's no threat). The lack of pressure is the test — if desire surfaces here, it's genuine, not coerced.
